description: Mexican drug cartel founded in 1996 by the five Beltrán Leyva brothers, originally part of the Sinaloa Cartel. It trafficked cocaine, heroin, and marijuana, and engaged in human smuggling, money laundering, extortion, kidnapping, and murder. The cartel infiltrated Mexican government agencies and Interpol. It was declared disbanded in 2011 after the arrest of its last major lieutenant, though remnants continued fighting until 2017.

country_links.evidence_quote: Operations in Mexico City, Chiapas, Guerrero, Guanajuato, Mexico State, Morelos, Nuevo León, Querétaro, Quintana Roo, Sinaloa, Sonora, and Tamaulipas, as well as in the United States and Canada;
country_links.context: The organization operated in Canada, as indicated by its assets and operations.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Beltrán Leyva brothers' Colombian cocaine supplier, Ever Villafane Martínez, was arrested in Morelos in August 2008.
country_links.context: The organization sourced cocaine from Colombian suppliers.

country_links.evidence_quote: Operations in Mexico City, Chiapas, Guerrero, Guanajuato, Mexico State, Morelos, Nuevo León, Querétaro, Quintana Roo, Sinaloa, Sonora, and Tamaulipas, as well as in the United States and Canada;
country_links.context: The organization operated in the United States, as indicated by its assets and operations.
